item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who said no feathers? Can't demons have feathers?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this is an interesting concept and is well executed for the most part, but journals and written documents transcribing patterns of speech in this way is a real pet peeve for me. This is a man writing for no one but himself, and he definitely wouldn't put in apostrophes for missing letters because of how he pronounces those words when spoken. Instead, a contemporary journal would likely be rife with spelling errors as well as spelling and word usage uncommon today.

People do write the way they talk, in that they often use colloquialisms and common rhythms of speech, but they don't spell their 'accent' on paper. A little more grittiness and less 'howdy varmint!' in the tone of these entries would make this one a win for me. As someone else suggested, journals from this time period are your best resource for writing material of this kind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quoting Essenn on this: cowboys shooting dinosaurs and people fucking dying due to O2 poisoning or going into a religious madness? Oh fuck yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, basically, they thought that they were in hell surrounded by demons just because they didn't know any better.

A thought-provoking article. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly its an interesting idea, but it needs more information about how the foundation discovered that it time traveled, that and I think the "no feathers" thing is kinda dumb.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The revolver image used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC-BY-SA 3.0) due to it being from the Wichita Eagle which is licensed with "NC". Thus, I have removed the image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think Eight-Year-Old Me might have had a joygasm at the thought of cowboys fighting dinosaurs (and especially a T-Rex getting it’s head blown off). Meanwhile, Grown-Up Me loves the (to borrow from TV Tropes) “Reality Ensues” approach to a western town time-transported back to the Late Cretaceous Era; mass deaths from not just dinosaurs but the unfit-for-humans atmosphere, religious freak outs galore, and a poor, simple just man trying to comprehend exactly what’s happened to him and everything he once knew.

Enjoy your +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! We're really glad you enjoyed!

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded: I am (fashionably) late to this, but I agree as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is probably my favorite Series V article. I love everything about it.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded: Sounds OK to us!

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

This article already has a pair of crosslinks, but as I reviewed it and other anomalies, I found one other similar anomaly which may be of interest: SCP-3834. Both of these anomalies are anachronistic, from the Cretaceous, and found in Montana.

Changing this:

Description: SCP-4003 is the group designation for the archaeological remains of the town of Temperance and its citizens found in the Hell Creek Formation near Jordan, Montana. Fossils, objects, and remains found within SCP-4003 date to the upper Cretaceous era.

To this:

Description: SCP-4003 is the group designation for the archaeological remains of the town of Temperance and its citizens found in the Hell Creek Formation near Jordan, Montana. Fossils, objects, and remains found within SCP-4003 date to the upper Cretaceous era1.

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

Footnotes
1. Due to the roughly similar location of discovery and dated time of origin, investigation of a connection between SCP-4003 and SCP-3834 is underway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: lmao

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Firstly, your critique shouldn’t be hidden with white text. It just makes it harder for the author to read it (just to clarify I am not the author).

Secondly, this really isn’t good reasoning for your downvote. Your reasoning for downvoting boils down to “The dinosaurs don’t have feathers”, which the article doesn’t even confirm whether or not the dinosaurs have feathers. If you liked the plot, and your reasoning for downvoting doesn’t make sense, then why downvote?

It’s fine to dislike something, but hiding what you don’t like about it and it being something nonsensical doesn’t help the author in any way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's just plain inaccurate. Dinosaurs had feathers, so deal with it. However, it still has a good plot.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm impressed by how much of a fun romp this is. Take the +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kind of a cliche idea, whole town going back in time, but the execution more than made up for it. +1.

hey, that's pretty neat.

thanks for reading!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I started reading this I was like "meh, just another time travelling scp" but the journal Was what made it a +1 for me. Really well written. Just one thing: in the journal, is it meant to be vermint or is it a spelling mistake of Vermin?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice idea! Some suggestions:

Following its disappearance, Temperance and its population manifested in the late Cretaceous era about 66 million years ago. It is believed most, if not all of the inhabitants and livestock perished from oxygen poisoning due to the increased levels of the chemical compound present in the Earth's atmosphere at the time. Any survivors were then likely to have perished from exposure, disease, famine, and the predatory megafauna.

The town of Temperance reappeared in its exact geographical location in the Upper Cretaceous Period. The lithology of SCP-4003 and the surrounding area indicate the climate of the area was subtropical due to its proximity to the Western Interior Seaway; sharply contrasting the aridity of modern Montana.

These two paragraphs seem redundant together, maybe find a way to combine them?

Though originally believed to be Homo neanderthalensis specimens, the anatomy of the found skulls were identical to that of a Homo sapiens.

It might be good to address the fact that Neanderthal remains have never been discovered in America, so these would have been the only known American Neanderthal remains had they indeed been Neanderthal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's very close and its a lot brighter than the moon. The light is moving too: it's only next to the moon for a day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok so I have a question. How is the Big God Light shinin' next to the moon also visible during the day? Unless it's orbiting the earth, it would rise and set at roughly the same pace as the sun, so if it's visible at night, how is it still in the same position in the sky during the day?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The picture is most definitely not a T-rex, I'm pretty sure it's not even a tyrannosauroid at all.
This is a picture of juvenile T-rex skull, and it gets bulkier with age. If I had to guess I'd say it was some sort of allosauroid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice! I'd love to see these extended logs, tbh. (Yes, the SCP is already highly rated, but I've found (such as with SCP-003) making a great SCP even better has corresponding long-term rating rewards.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ugh it's dumb and good. Very nice.

item.title: Re: 4003!
item.content:encoded: This has been my biggest contribution to the foundation. It was amazing watching Nico's idea grow and it was even more amazing when he let me be a part of it. Congrats to everyone, this has truly been a historic journey!

What a long journey this has been. Thanks to all who read our article and congratulations to all the contest entrants!
Here's to another ten years of the Foundation, and maybe some more dinosaur articles. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really good and a lot of fun. I especially loved the little detail about the Hypnosuggestive agents. I only have a few small issues with it. With the safe being anomalously preserved as it was, it might have been nice to have some link in there that indicated why the town was time-travelled. Otherwise, it just feels like a bit of random "needs to happen to the journal can exist" forced weirdness.

Secondly, this paragraph:

It is believed most, if not all of the inhabitants and livestock perished from oxygen poisoning due to the increased levels of the chemical compound present in the Earth's atmosphere at the time. Any survivors were then likely to have perished from exposure, disease, famine, and the predatory megafauna.

You say that most or all of the inhabitants died of oxygen poisoning but that doesn't line up with what the journal says. Most of them were eaten by dinosaurs or ran off into the wilderness or whatever. Oxygen poisoning sets in relatively quickly from what I understand, certainly quicker than the weeks covered by this journal.

Other than those minor points, I really enjoyed this. Honestly I would have liked to have seen the journal be a bit longer, maybe have the survivors adapting a little to their new life or something before being killed by the asteroid. None the less, +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, I wanted to put a few more logs of the townsfolk finding and dealing with herbivores, I had a whole journal entry dedicated to talking about how a triceratops kinda looked like a buffalo if it also became part-lizard. As for the meteor, you are correct in that it did fall in Mexico. I originally had a few logs following the impact with things like 'Father Jacob didn't say Hell would be so cold' and such, but the time crunch of the contest got to me. x_x

I did want to further expand on the journals, which I may do once the contest is over and this has a proper spot on the main list.

I'm happy to hear you liked it though. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this a lot.

I have a major suggested change, though, which is that they should be seeing way, way more herbivores than predators. I don't mind suspending disbelief for some suicidal hyper-predators, but I think it would be lots of fun to expand the logs so that they get to see a bunch of the weird-ass herbivore dinosaurs herds (who also have no fear of humans, naturally) as well as successfully drive off some of the predators simply by injuring them. If you're looking for an Old West metaphor, think herds of bison & cattle (or for a potentially more accurate metaphor, the mixed-species herds from African grasslands).

Also, the meteor didn't fall on Montana, so if that's what is being referenced, you could expand the logs to include the aftermath of the strike down in Mexico. (BTW, haven't recently googled this, so double-check that info to make sure it's right!)

item.title: Re: No.
item.content:encoded: I've fixed this, thank you for the heads up.

item.title: Re: Team Dino Desperado proudly presents our 4K entry!
item.content:encoded:
It's true that 1850s-era daguerrotypes are public domain and you can do what you want with them.

It's still good practice to give their provenance, though, since anyone can say that something is public domain.

  • "Temperance's Main Street, circa 1866" is https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Fairbank_year_1890.jpg
  • "Temperance and her first citizens"- July 5th, anno Domini 1859" is https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lynch's_Slave_Market_by_Thomas_Easterly,_c1852.png
  • "Reminder that I paid too much for a photograph" is https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:-Gentleman-_MET_DP218306.jpg
  • "A daguerreotype photograph found folded within the journal" is https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:-Man_in_a_Sheraton_Chair-_MET_39.22.2.jpg

item.title: No.
item.content:encoded:
most, if not all of the inhabitants and livestock perished from carbon monoxide poisoning due to the increased levels of the chemical compound present in the Earth's atmosphere at the time

that's not how carbon monoxide works. In an oxygen atmosphere — especially when you're not in a building — carbon monoxide oxidizes to carbon dioxide pretty quickly. Without civilization to provide constant sources of monoxide, it's not plausible that there'd be toxic levels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article reminded me of the tv show Terra Nova, in which colonists have to live in the Cretaceous period due to future Earth becoming polluted. Nevertheless I found your skip pretty refreshing to read and it never feels like a drag, unlike some other entries.

+1

item.title: DONT WEAR COWBOY HATS AT 3 AM (ALMOST DIED?!?) (MET A DINOSAUR!!)
item.content:encoded: dinosaurs are epic and im glad i drew a dinosaur today thank you for letting me have this opportunity

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ONE OF US ONE OF US

This is supposed to be a joke.

hey that's pretty good.

Thanks for reading, folks!

EDIT- Some art to commemorate!

Mady by pastarasta1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had a feeling that you linked to TV Tropes before I clicked on that link. I think I spent a lot of time on TV Tropes.

item.title: Re: Technical Captain Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks for clearing that up, we were a little confused when seagull added that.

item.title: Technical Captain Post
item.content:encoded: It is not, and I have re-added it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is "town" a valid tag? There is only one page (which is this one) with the tag "town".
Also, why isn't there a "co-authored" tag..

item.title: Dinosaur Advice
item.content:encoded:
Kids, do not attempt to pray the dinosaurs away.

No matter how much you love Jesus, they'll still kill you.

Christian, Jewish, Buddhist or Atheist, they'll still eat you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be honest, this is the best praise I could have ever asked for. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is incredibly stupid and the ending is as contrived as it gets and I could not stop grinning ear to ear as I read.

Bravo!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neat.

While I think you missed an opportunity to full force with western settlers hunting down dinosaurs, the darker approach here is far more fitting to the Foundationverse, and you do still have a T-Rex get its head blown off with dynamite which is a true spectacle in and of itself.

I'll echo what was said above about the journal in that it is to an authentic journal what butter is to margarine, a decent imitation but still a little off. I don't imagine all the speaking quirks would be there, and as a result, it reads more like a transcript.

Still, for an article about time traveling cowboys fighting dinosaurs, its a solid job.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this, just something fun and simple, which we certainly need right now, although I'm not sure if its grand enough to be a 4000, but I would definitely read more +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We definitely have a head canon on what it is though… There are clues if you look closely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The perfect way to end it, in my opinion.

A scared man watching Chicxulub making its steady approach, mistaking impending armageddon for a sign of hope and peace.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
why would you link tv tropes oh god what have you done
That aside, I agree with you. This article lives and dies by the rule of cool, and I definitely took some artistic licenses here. I'm glad you did not find them to be a detriment.
As for the wooden bits of the guns, I have implemented your advice. The conditions for them to be petrified just aren't there, so they're decayed now! Thanks for your help, I appreciate it immensely. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It might be.
It may also have been something else.
Or it may have been God Himself.
Shame there's no journal on what it is!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw His light in the sky, shinin' so bright next to the moon.

I see Him in the sky in the day now too. He's so bright behind these clouds, I can feel His touch.
But God ain't happy with me. I know my punishment is comin', and God almighty himself is deliverin' it.

Chicxulub, is that you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was expecting something like riding dinosaurs in the Wild West, but this would suffice. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
love it, we need more "whimsical oddity" SCPs.

The depiction of the dinos suffers a bit of the Artistic License – Paleontology effect with small suicidal dinos attacking prey larger than themselves (see: rarity of wolf or lion predation on humans). And bigger dinos not running away from guns like modern mega-fauna. But rule of cool reigns supreme here so it doesn't detract from the story for me.

Otherwise the recovery notes for the firearms are a little odd.

Revolvers have a metal frame, which wooden grips are attached to like plates. So it makes more sense for the wood to have decayed away rather than have "broken off."

I'd note the "decay" for the rifles too, as both the stock and barrel guard are made of wood and would have decayed by now unless they underwent petrification, which is a rather specific process

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a lot of fun to work on! I'm beyond words with how happy the reception has made me, I'm glad so many people are enjoying our piece. Thanks a bunch for the nice words and helpful critique, everything helps us improve as we move forward and it's much appreciated. <3

item.title: Re: Team Dino Desperado proudly presents our 4K entry!
item.content:encoded: This really was an amazing project to have worked on so on behalf of team Dino Desperado, please enjoy!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for the concept and the theme. The written logs don’t exactly ring true- they look more like a transcription of speech than like something that a person from that time period would have written. I’d suggest that you read letters and journal entries from that actual time period to get a sense of the writing style.

Still, +1. This is a fun article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really nice stuff. Great journal. You painted a vivid picture of the situation through a well-realized period character, without extraneous bells and whistles. Just what I like to see from a history contest :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love your entry, and I'm flattered you like mine as well! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Easily one of the top two Catholicism-related SCP-4000 contest entries so far. Love the premise and the way it shakes out for the townsfolk. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad you liked it, and I'm flattered to have fed your canon. :D
As per the spelling errors and missing words, only two of the ones you pointed out were intentional. Everything else has been fixed, along with the specimen situation.
Thank you very much!
